Review is on hold because of differing opinions from the oom and policy framework committers regarding the generation of passwords for secrets.

Patch Set 5:

@Krzysztof, @Sylvain, we are facing some issues complying with the comments of randomizing passwords for previously existing api and pap components as they affect existing code.   Given the broad scope of the changes, the tight istanbul timeline, and the time that it takes to pass the gating process, it is difficult to satisfy.   There are some big issues for us as it broadens the scope of the work: 1) the gating testsuites use the default passwords for api and pap to pass the gating, and they fail with 401 failures for the tests.  2) other code bases besides policy may need to change, I think oof or dcae defaults will have to change to grab the same generated passwords as well, not sure if there would impact on basic_clamp testsuite as well.   This implies quite a bit of work and coodination with other teams.  Due to these reasons, and time constraints, the ask to the OOM team is for relief on istanbul so to be able to keep the default passwords (but having the default password generation randomized infrastructure).  We can work with OOM team in early jakarta to move in the proposed direction.   What do you suggest?
Removed Code-Review-1 by Ramesh Murugan Iyer <ramesh.murugan.iyer@est.tech>

The issue is syncing all places in OOM and related projects like testsuites with the random password. 
For example, you can see that the OOM gating tests failed in this review with "unauthorized - 401" most likely because of it:

Basic Policy Health Check                                             | FAIL |
401 != 200
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Enhanced Policy New Healthcheck                                       | FAIL |
401 != 200

In general, any component that has been interacting with the existing API and PAP policy components using the default password for the last few releases will have to change to make sure are updated with the newly generated ones.
